source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ CE MDD/■ 4. 4차 CE 인증 자료/07. Usability Engineering/A-UEP-001 Usability Engineering Plan 2020.12.10.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Plans usability engineering activities, responsibilities, risk permission criteria, validation scenarios, intended users, and training considerations for the detector.
keyPoints: The document is intended to plan the usability of the detector HAD1717MG,HAD1717MC.
HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.
Not to be used for mammography and infant or pregnant women.
Validation is performed to assess and reduce the risk posed by correct use and usability related errors through the usability engineering process.
Typical intended users are medical personnel with professional qualifications who are familiar with the User Manual and can understand Korean.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability engineering plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.

evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG usability engineering plan

evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-UEP-001 (Rev.4)

evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Lee, Dong wook DEC/12/2020 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin DEC/12/2020 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu DEC/12/2020

evidence.claim: Revision 4 is dated DEC/12/2020 and records correction of audit points.

evidence.claim: The document cites EN ISO 14971:2012 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2012, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document cites EN 62366:2008 for usability engineering of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366:2008 - Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are intended for general radiographic digital imaging of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The devices are intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The devices are not to be used for mammography and infant or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and infant or pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The patient population includes radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, chest, spinal, abdominal, hand, foot, and other internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs.

evidence.claim: The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license who is familiar with the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: A medical professional with a professional license Knowledge: Conditions that satisfy both of the following two conditions - A medical professional with a professional license - familiar with user manual

evidence.claim: The intended user's language understanding is Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Language Understanding: Korean

evidence.claim: The operating condition limit is ambient temperature +5 to 40℃ and relative humidity 30 to 75 RH% without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:+5 ~ 40℃ - Relative humidity:30 ~ 75 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The storage condition limit is ambient temperature -10 to +50℃ and relative humidity 10 to 80 RH% without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:-10 ~ + 50℃ - Relative humidity:10 ~ 80 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The document states ESL is 5 years based on frequency-of-use assumptions.
evidence.evidenceText: 1year= 2,400h 1year= 50weeks ∴ ESL = 5 years
evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG expected service life

evidence.claim: The classification of applied parts is Not Applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification of APPLIED PARTS Not Applied part
evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG applied parts classification

evidence.claim: Usability validation is performed to assess and reduce risks from correct use and usability-related errors.
evidence.evidenceText: Validation is performed to assess and reduce the risk posed by correct use and usability related errors through the usability engineering process of the detector HAD1717MG (HAD17MC).

evidence.claim: The usability test staff consists of 5 typical intended users.
evidence.evidenceText: Typical Intended Users (Test Staff) : 5 persons

evidence.claim: The acceptable risk criteria define Level 1 as widely acceptable and Levels 2 through 16 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone, Widely Acceptable Zone: [Level 1] Red Zone, Inacceptable Zone: [Level 2~16]

evidence.claim: Risk control alternatives include inherent safety by design, preventive measures, and information on stability.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Control Alternatives 1 inherent safety by design 2 Preventive measures for medical devices themselves or manufacturing processes 3 information on stability

evidence.claim: Primary motor functions for usability validation include connecting the power cord, cable code connection, main power button action, PC software settings, and x-ray irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: primary motor function Connecting the power cord Cable Code Connection Main Power Button Action PC Software Settings x-ray irradiation
evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG usability validation plan

evidence.claim: Accompanying documents include Risk Management Plan, Risk Management Report, Usability Engineering Report, and TCF.
evidence.evidenceText: Accompanying document - Risk Management Plan (A-RMP-5-01) - Risk Management Report(A-RMR-5-01) - Usability Engineering Report (A-UER-001) - TCF (TCF-001)

evidence.claim: If training is required for safe and effective use of a primary operating function, the manufacturer shall provide training materials, ensure materials are available, or provide the training.
evidence.evidenceText: If training on the specific MEDICAL DEVICE is required for the safe and effective use of a PRIMARYOPERATING FUNCTION by the intended USER, the MANUFACTURER shall do at least one of the following: – provide the materials necessary for training – ensure that the materials necessary for training are available – provide the training.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly identified in the source text; source metadata authoritySignal is FDA while sourcePath indicates CE MDD.
The sourcePath filename contains 2020.12.10, but the source text shows DEC/12/2020 for preparation, review, approval, and Rev.4; documentDate uses the source text date.
Text contains apparent transcription or wording issues such as HAD17MC, PRIMARYOPERATING, primary motility, Inacceptable Zone, and x-ray investigation.
